theme: Generation engine — a cheap-LLM batch profile generator: a YAML batch descriptor -> realistic per-member profiles via gpt-4o-mini, with parallel/throttled generation, a prompt-hash cache, and the CODE-owns-structure / AI-owns-content boundary enforced by the existing taxonomy closure gate. Components (built inside-out, fixtures-first, TOK-01): (1) services/ai/ wrapper over the shared `ai` lib (EU-first routing -> 429 fallback -> model->price cost tracking; values-blind); (2) blueprint.Batch + batch[] on Story/StackSeed + EffectiveBatches() (pure Go-template mother-prompt expansion, NO LLM at parse time -> deterministic $0-reseed foundation); (3) the batchcache/ prompt-hash cache (atomic .tmp->rename, .lock fence, capture-version invalidation); (4) cmd/gen-batch (gpt-4o-mini default, MANDATORY --max-cost ceiling, --max-concurrent semaphore, --call-timeout, re-roll-on-malformed + hero-collision re-roll, per-batch cost report); (5) the GeneratedBatchSeeder (surface 'generated-batch', DependsOn users+taxonomy, PerStackIsolated; cache -> rows via the existing resolvers, drop-not-fabricate). The FIRST new third-party dep in the seeding module (ai v1.40.1 — a deliberate, user-acknowledged in-release supply-chain inflection; v1.8->v1.10 was 0-new-deps). Entirely in rosetta-extensions — ZERO platform-repo edits.

gate.target: On a real batch of N: valid-JSON >=95% (re-roll on malformed), every role/skill name resolves to a REAL public-taxonomy node-id via the existing resolvers (non-resolving drop, closure green), ZERO generated name collides with a hand-curated hero, total cost <= --max-cost; reproducible byte-identical from cache at $0.
gate.achieved: ALL 5 DIMENSIONS PASS on a REAL Azure gpt-4o-mini run (EU-first Sweden AZURE_API_KEY+AZURE_ENDPOINT; the direct OPENAI_KEY was billing-dead/429), N=20 + a fresh demo-3 proof: (1) valid-JSON 100.0% over 33 calls (the extra calls are name-dedup re-rolls) >= 95% PASS; (2) taxonomy-resolution 47/47 claimed skills + 20/20 roles -> REAL skiller.*.node_id (resolvers SELECT node_id, e.g. K-PYTHON-8B21), datadna measure-closure --stack demo-3 = [PASS] (closure GREEN, 0 fabrication) PASS; (3) 0 hero-collisions ('Maya Chen' never generated) PASS; (4) cost $0.0059 of the $0.10 ceiling (5.9%) PASS; (5) $0 byte-identical re-seed: run 2 -> 0 calls / $0.0000 / 20 cache hits, cache bytes unchanged PASS.

iter_ledger.tiks: iter-02..iter-07 (6 tiks); budget 3-5 iters, re-scope trigger (cheap model can't reach threshold after ~5 tiks) NOT fired — the gate was met on the first real run.
iter_ledger.summary: iter-01 tok: authored ai-generation-spec.md + cache-spec.md + TOK-01. iter-02..06: built components (1)-(5) inside-out, fixtures-first, each unit-proven with NO key (services/ai -> blueprint.Batch -> batchcache -> cmd/gen-batch -> GeneratedBatchSeeder); engine CODE-COMPLETE at iter-06. iter-07: the FIRST real LLM run -> gate 0/5 -> 5/5 MET; surfaced+fixed 3 issues (per-call --call-timeout stall-class, intra-batch name dedup via prompt+re-roll-hint -> 20/20 distinct names, user_skills CHECK 23514 -> ONE current-role experience per gen member) + an Azure env-name EU-first resolution fix.

Coverage-as-finder, not goal.
